receiptIds.description: Array of sales receipt UUIDs to email (1–200)
Array of sales receipt UUIDs to generate PDFs for (1–100)
Array of sales receipt UUIDs to mark as fully collected (1–200)
Array of sales receipt UUIDs to void (1–50)

isActive.description: Whether the account is active. Defaults to true on create; toggle via PATCH or DELETE.

onlinePayment.description: Whether the account is enabled for online payments

isActive.description: Whether the bank is active. Defaults to true on create; toggle via PATCH or DELETE.

name.description: Display name for the branch — must not match another branch in your organization.

isActive.description: Whether the cash register is active. Defaults to true on create; toggle via PATCH or DELETE.

name.description: Display name for the certificate. Defaults to `Certificado {currentYear}` when blank.

isOwn.description: True if it is an own check (issued); false for third-party checks

bankAccountId.description: Bank account id this checkbook belongs to

numberFrom.description: First check number in the book

numberTo.description: Last check number in the book

bankAccountId.description: Bank account id linked to this card

businessName.description: Customer's legal/business name. Required unless a valid `taxId` (CUIT) is provided — in that case it is auto-filled from ARCA's padrón.

documentTypeId.description: Document type id (combo). Fetch valid ids via `GET /combos/2/items`. When omitted, it is inferred from `taxId`: a valid 11-digit CUIT resolves to CUIT, 7–8 numeric digits resolve to DNI, anything else resolves to "Sin identificar".

ignoresTaxes.description: When true, IVA is ignored on receipts for this customer

providerId.description: External provider identifier. Unique per organization; acts as an idempotency key.

saleConditionId.description: Default sale condition (combo) for this customer

taxCategoryId.description: Tax category id (combo). Must belong to the organization's regime — fetch valid ids via `GET /tax-categories`. Required unless a valid `taxId` (CUIT) is provided — in that case it is inferred from the customer's IVA condition in ARCA combined with the organization's regime.

taxId.description: Tax ID (CUIT/CUIL/DNI). When 11 digits, validated as CUIT.

taxIdNumber.description: Secondary tax id number, when applicable

accountId.description: Bank account id receiving the deposit

path.description: Storage path of the file to import (must already exist).

type.description: Import type. Must match the file's contents.

accessToken.description: Mercado Pago access token (returned by OAuth).

createInvoice.description: When true, an invoice is automatically created on successful Mercado Pago payments.

includePaymentButton.description: Whether to include a Mercado Pago payment button on emailed receipts (e.g. ALWAYS, NEVER, OPTIONAL).

publicKey.description: Public key for the MP integration

receiptBookId.description: Receipt book to use for auto-created invoices.

refreshToken.description: Mercado Pago refresh token (returned by OAuth).

isActive.description: Whether the payment method is active. Defaults to true on create; toggle via PATCH or DELETE.

paymentMethodTypeId.description: Payment method type combo item id

externalEntityId.description: External entity id (if linked to a receipt or other entity)

categoryId.description: Product category ID; null/0 may resolve to Sin Rubro

providerId.description: External provider identifier. Unique per organization; acts as an idempotency key.

unitPrice.description: Default unit price for auto-populating receipt lines

description.description: Display name for the receipt book.

isExport.description: When true, routes to WSFEX (export) instead of WSFE (domestic).

pointOfSale.description: AFIP point of sale (Punto de Venta) number. Unique per organization.

prefix.description: Prefix used to compose receipt numbers (e.g. `0001-00000123`). Unique per organization.

type.description: Point of sale type (WS = AFIP web service, WS_MOCK = synthetic, EN_LINEA = online, PRE_IMPRESOS = printed).

accountId.description: Account id used for the retention

isActive.description: Whether the retention is active. Defaults to true on create; toggle via PATCH or DELETE.

isValue.description: Whether the retention is treated as a value (vs percentage)

exportData.description: Export-specific data for WSFEX invoices (e.g. incoterms)

isDraft.description: Create as draft (BORRADOR) without triggering AFIP

providerId.description: External provider identifier. Unique per organization; acts as an idempotency key.

serviceEndDate.description: Service period end date (required for service organizations)

serviceStartDate.description: Service period start date (required for service organizations)

businessName.description: Supplier's legal/business name. Required unless a valid `taxId` (CUIT) is provided — in that case it is auto-filled from ARCA's padrón.

documentTypeId.description: Document type id (combo). Fetch valid ids via `GET /combos/2/items`. When omitted, it is inferred from `taxId`: a valid 11-digit CUIT resolves to CUIT, 7–8 numeric digits resolve to DNI, anything else resolves to "Sin identificar".

purchaseConditionId.description: Purchase condition (combo) id. Fetch valid ids via `GET /combos/CondicionCompraVenta/items`.

taxCategoryId.description: Tax category id. Must belong to the organization's regime — fetch valid ids via `GET /tax-categories`. Required unless a valid `taxId` (CUIT) is provided — in that case it is inferred from the supplier's IVA condition in ARCA combined with the organization's regime.

taxId.description: Tax ID (CUIT). When 11 digits, validated as CUIT.

code.description: Numeric AFIP error code (e.g. 10016 for already-authorized). Only present on AFIP errors that carry a numeric code.

details.description: Field-level validation reasons. Populated for validation errors and structured business errors.

error.description: SCREAMING_SNAKE_CASE enum-style error code. Always machine-mappable. Examples: AFIP_PERSONA_NOT_FOUND, AFIP_WSCI_ERROR, INVALID_CUIT, DB_UNIQUE_VIOLATION, NOT_FOUND, BAD_REQUEST, UNAUTHORIZED.

message.description: Developer-friendly English description of the error. Always present.
message.example: No AFIP taxpayer found for CUIT 20999999999.

path.description: Request path that triggered the error.

providerMessage.description: Raw message from the upstream provider (AFIP, etc.) with envelope noise stripped. Present on errors that wrap a third-party failure; kept in the provider's original language.
providerMessage.example: No existe persona con ese Id

requestId.description: pino-http request ID for log correlation.

timestamp.description: ISO-8601 timestamp of when the error occurred.

onlinePayment.description: Whether the account is enabled for online payments

info.description:
# Peakly API

The Peakly API provides programmatic access to invoicing, customers, products, and more.

## Authentication

Authenticate using an API key in the `X-API-Key` header:
```
X-API-Key: pk_...
```

API keys are scoped to an organization — the organization context is resolved automatically from the key.

Create and manage API keys via the `/v1/api-keys` endpoints.
info.title: Peakly API

parameters.description: Filter by module (ventas, compras, tesoreria, manual)
parameters.name: module

get.summary: Get a journal entry with its items
Daily Book (Libro Diario) — chronological journal entries
General Ledger (Libro Mayor) — account movements with running balance
Trial Balance (Balance de Sumas y Saldos) — account totals

get.description: Get the list of active branches to populate branch pickers and decide which point of sale to use. Deleted branches are not included.

post.description: Create a branch for each physical location your business operates from. Once created, you can reference its id on receipts and assign point-of-sale numbers to it. The name must be different from your other branches.

delete.description: Stop a branch from appearing in pickers and reports when you no longer operate from it. Past receipts that reference the branch keep working.

get.description: Get a branch's details — useful when you've stored an id on a receipt and need to display the branch name and address.

patch.description: Edit the branch — for example to fix the address or update the contact phone. Send only the fields you want to change. If you change `name`, it still has to be different from your other branches.

get.description: Get every certificate associated with your organization, including ones that are still being set up. Use `GET /certificates/active` when you only need the one currently in use for invoicing.

post.description: Begin the certificate setup flow. POST with a `name` and the API will generate a CSR for you. Next, download the CSR via `GET /certificates/:id/csr`, upload it to AFIP, and post the `.crt` AFIP gives back via `POST /certificates/:id/upload-crt`.

201.description: Certificate created — download the CSR next

get.description: Get the certificate currently used to authorize receipts with AFIP. A 404 means you don't have a valid certificate set up — receipts will still be created but will stay in `PENDIENTE_AFIP` until you complete the certificate flow (`POST /certificates` → upload CSR to AFIP → `POST /certificates/:id/upload-crt`).

Use this to drive an expiration banner or renewal reminder in your UI. For each certificate you get back days remaining, whether it's expired, and whether it's ready to use. Plan renewals ahead of time — AFIP issuance can take a few days.

delete.description: Remove a certificate you no longer need (e.g. an expired one you've already replaced). If you delete the active certificate before uploading a replacement, receipts can still be created but will stay in `PENDIENTE_AFIP` — they won't be authorized by AFIP until a new certificate is active.

get.description: Get a certificate's name, status, and expiration date. To download the CSR or CRT file, use the `/csr` and `/crt` sub-paths.

patch.description: Rename a certificate or toggle `isActive` to switch which certificate is used for invoicing. Only one should be active at a time.

get.description: Download the AFIP-signed `.crt` for this certificate — useful for backups or to inspect when the certificate expires.

404.description: Certificate or CRT file not found

get.description: Download the CSR file for this certificate so you can upload it to AFIP's certificate portal. Once AFIP gives you back a `.crt`, finish setup by posting it via `POST /certificates/:id/upload-crt`.

404.description: Certificate or CSR file not found

post.description: Finish setting up the certificate by uploading the `.crt` AFIP gave you when you submitted the CSR. After this call, the certificate is ready to use — run `POST /certificates/:id/validate-connection` to double-check.

crt.description: The `.crt` file you downloaded from AFIP.

200.description: Certificate is now ready to use

400.description: No file provided, or the file isn't a valid certificate

post.description: Run a quick check against AFIP to confirm the certificate works for both domestic invoicing (WSFE) and export invoicing (WSFEX) before you rely on it for real receipts. The response tells you if each service authenticated, and if not, the error message AFIP returned. Run this right after uploading a CRT.

200.description: `{ success, environment, services: { wsfe, wsfex } }`. `success` is `true` only when both services pass.

400.description: You need to upload the CRT first

get.description: Returns an empty list — combos are fetched by id. Use `GET /combos/:id/items` with the id of the list you need (5 for receipt types, 6 for sale conditions, etc.).
Get the combo's name and description. To list the items the user can pick from, call `GET /combos/:id/items` instead.

Fetch the items of a combo to populate a dropdown. Show `data` to the user; send the item's `id` when creating a receipt or customer that needs this value. The IVA combo is filtered automatically based on your organization's tax regime — Monotributista organizations only see *Sin IVA*.

parameters.description: Combo id (e.g. 5 = receipt types, 6 = sale conditions)
parameters.name: id

200.description: Object with list of favorite currency ids

get.description: Returns a page of customers. Use cursor from nextCursor for the next page.

parameters.description: Filter by business name, tax ID, or email (partial match)
parameters.name: search

parameters.description: Exact match on the external provider identifier
parameters.name: provider_id

parameters.description: Filter by business name, tax ID, or email (partial match)
parameters.name: search

parameters.description: Exact match on the external provider identifier
parameters.name: provider_id

get.description: Validates CUIT and fetches business data from AFIP via system certificate.

parameters.description: CUIT to look up (11 digits, with or without dashes)
parameters.name: cuit

400.description: INVALID_CUIT — malformed or bad check digit.

404.description: AFIP_PERSONA_NOT_FOUND — no taxpayer registered in AFIP for this CUIT.

502.description: AFIP_WSCI_ERROR — upstream AFIP (WSCI) failure. See providerMessage.

get.description: Get the exchange rate to use when invoicing in a foreign currency. Pass the target currency (`to`) and use the rate from the response. The response is `null` when no rate is available for that day — show your user a manual-entry input or block the operation when that happens.

parameters.description: Source currency id. Omit to use your organization's default currency.
parameters.name: from

parameters.description: ISO date — defaults to today. Use the date the receipt is issued on.
parameters.name: date

200.description: Exchange rate, or `null` when none is available

400.description: Missing `to`, or `to`/`from`/`date` is invalid

get.description: Get a signed URL (valid for 1 hour) to download a file the API generated for you — for example, a CSV export or a bulk PDF job. Pass the `key` you received from the export endpoint and download the file from the `url` you get back. You can only download files that belong to your own organization.

parameters.description: The export key returned by whichever endpoint generated the file.
parameters.name: key

200.description: `{ url }` — fetch this within 1 hour to download the file

400.description: Missing or invalid key, or the file has expired

get.summary: Get a download URL for an exported file
List file imports for the current organization

400.description: Invalid key

get.summary: Get signed download URL for an import result file

get.description: Download a pre-built Excel template with headers, example rows, and valid value hints. Type must be one of: customers, products, sales-receipts.

400.description: Invalid template type

post.description: Upload an Excel (.xlsx, .xls) or CSV (.csv) file for import processing. Creates a FileImport record and stores the file server-side for asynchronous processing.

201.description: File uploaded and import record created

400.description: Invalid file or type

get.summary: Get payment method entry by id

get.description: Get the list of locations to populate the location dropdown when creating a customer, branch, or receipt. Returns Argentine locations by default. Pass `onlyExport=true` when the user is issuing an export invoice and needs to pick a foreign destination. Use `nombre` to narrow the list as the user types.

parameters.description: Filter by name (case-insensitive partial match) — pair with the user's search input
parameters.name: nombre

parameters.description: Pass `true` to get foreign destinations for export invoicing. Omit (or `false`) for Argentine locations.
parameters.name: onlyExport

get.description: Get the location to pre-select on new customer/branch forms. Returns 404 when no default has been configured — fall back to letting the user pick from the full list.

patch.description: Pick the location that should be pre-selected on new customer/branch forms. Only one location is the default at a time — calling this replaces the previous one.

parameters.description: Location id to set as default
parameters.name: id

patch.summary: Set a location as the default

get.description: Get a single location's details — useful when you have an id stored on a customer/branch/receipt and need to display its name.

get.summary: List Mercado Pago configs for the current organization
Get or create the Mercado Pago config for the current organization
Get Mercado Pago config by id

patch.summary: Reset auth — clears the stored refresh token so the user must reconnect

parameters.description: Filter by category name (description, partial match)
parameters.name: name

get.summary: List product categories for the current organization

get.description: Returns a page of products. Use cursor from nextCursor for the next page.

parameters.description: Filter by product name (description, partial match)
parameters.name: name

parameters.description: Exact match on the external provider identifier
parameters.name: provider_id

parameters.description: Filter by description or barcode (partial match)
parameters.name: search

200.description: Search results with favorites, recents, and matches

get.summary: Search products with favorites, recents, and fuzzy matching

post.summary: Toggle product as favorite for the organization

parameters.description: Filter by stock change type (SALE, PURCHASE, MANUAL)
parameters.name: type

get.summary: Get stock movement history for a product
List provider configs for the current organization

400.description: Organization has no secret_key or provider invalid

501.description: OAuth not implemented for this provider

get.summary: Get OAuth redirect URL for a provider
List purchase categories for the current organization

get.description: Returns a page of purchase receipts. Use cursor from nextCursor for the next page.

get.summary: Get unpaid receipts for a supplier (for payment orders)
Get purchase receipt by id (with details)

put.summary: Replace all details for a purchase receipt

get.description: Returns a page of suppliers. Use cursor from nextCursor for the next page.

parameters.description: Filter by business name, tax ID, or email (partial match)
parameters.name: search

parameters.description: Filter by business name, tax ID, or email (partial match)
parameters.name: search

post.description: Looks up supplier by CUIT. If not found, auto-creates using AFIP padron data.
post.summary: Get existing supplier by CUIT or create with AFIP data

get.description: Validates CUIT and fetches business data from AFIP via system certificate.

parameters.description: CUIT to look up (11 digits, with or without dashes)
parameters.name: cuit

400.description: INVALID_CUIT — malformed or bad check digit.

404.description: AFIP_PERSONA_NOT_FOUND — no taxpayer registered in AFIP for this CUIT.

502.description: AFIP_WSCI_ERROR — upstream AFIP (WSCI) failure. See providerMessage.

get.summary: List receipt books for the current organization
Get receipt book by point of sale

post.summary: Import puntos de venta from ARCA (AFIP)

parameters.description: Skip expensive per-receipt queries for high-volume orgs
parameters.name: summary_only

get.summary: Dashboard KPIs, charts, and top customers
Gross Income (IIBB) by jurisdiction and month
Export Sales by Customer as CSV
Export Sales by Product as CSV

parameters.description: Number of past months to include (default 24)
parameters.name: months

get.summary: Revenue trends with MoM/YoY comparisons and 3-month forecast
Export VAT Sales Book as CSV

get.description: Returns a page of sales receipts. Use cursor from nextCursor for the next page. If date_from/date_to are omitted, all dates are included.

parameters.description: Filter receipts on or after this date (ISO). Omit for all dates.
parameters.name: date_from

parameters.description: Filter receipts on or before this date (ISO). Omit for all dates.
parameters.name: date_to

parameters.description: Search by customer name, receipt number, or CAE. Supports prefixes: cae:<value>, cuit:<value>, nro:<value>, id:<value>
parameters.name: search

parameters.description: Exact match on the external provider identifier
parameters.name: provider_id

get.summary: List sales receipts (cursor-paginated, optional date filters)

400.description: Validation failed. Codes: ORGANIZATION_ARCHIVED, DETAILS_REQUIRED, MISSING_SALES_LETTER.

get.summary: Get bulk action job progress (email, PDF, or void)

400.description: Invalid receipt IDs

post.summary: Send emails for multiple receipts (background job)

400.description: Invalid receipt IDs

post.summary: Generate PDFs for multiple receipts as a ZIP file (background job)

400.description: Invalid receipt IDs

post.summary: Mark multiple receipts as fully collected (totalCollected = total)

400.description: Invalid receipt IDs

post.summary: Void multiple receipts as a background job (creates credit notes where required)

get.summary: List distinct users who created sales receipts for the org

parameters.description: Comma-separated column keys (overrides preset and saved config)
parameters.name: columns

get.summary: List all available export columns (key + header)

400.description: Validation failed. Codes: MISSING_SALES_LETTER.

get.summary: Preview the next receipt number for a receipt book + customer + type

delete.description: Hard-deletes a receipt and its details/observations. Borrador receipts can be deleted by any user. Pendiente_Afip receipts can only be deleted by root users. Creada receipts from non-WS (pre-impreso) receipt books can be deleted only if they are the last number in the receipt book. Confirmed WS receipts cannot be deleted — use a credit note instead.

400.description: Validation failed. Codes: ORGANIZATION_ARCHIVED, INVALID_RECEIPT_STATUS_FOR_DELETE, NOT_LAST_RECEIPT_NUMBER.

delete.summary: Delete a draft or stuck receipt

400.description: Validation failed. Codes: ORGANIZATION_ARCHIVED, RECEIPT_DATE_TOO_OLD, MISSING_SALES_LETTER.

post.description: Request AFIP authorization for a receipt that's in `PENDIENTE_AFIP`. The API normally drives this automatically when you call `POST /sales-receipts/:id/confirm` — call this endpoint directly only when you want to retry authorization on a receipt that previously failed.

400.description: Validation failed (or AFIP error). Codes: INVALID_RECEIPT_STATUS_FOR_AUTHORIZE.

post.description: Validates required fields, assigns real receipt number, sets status to Pendiente_Afip, and triggers AFIP authorization.

400.description: Validation failed. Codes: ORGANIZATION_ARCHIVED, INVALID_RECEIPT_STATUS_FOR_CONFIRM, CUSTOMER_REQUIRED, DETAILS_REQUIRED, RECEIPT_BOOK_REQUIRED, SALE_CONDITION_REQUIRED, RECEIPT_DATE_TOO_OLD, RECEIPT_BOOK_NOT_FOUND.

post.description: Creates a new BORRADOR draft by copying customer, items, conditions from the source receipt. Dates are reset to today. Any status receipt can be duplicated.
post.summary: Duplicate a receipt as a new draft (BORRADOR)

get.summary: Download invoice PDF via signed URL (public)

post.summary: Resend invoice email via expired signed link (public)

post.description: Clears any previous error and re-triggers AFIP authorization. Only receipts in Pendiente_Afip status can be retried.

400.description: Validation failed. Codes: INVALID_RECEIPT_STATUS_FOR_RETRY.

post.summary: Retry AFIP authorization for a pending receipt

200.description: Tracking events with tracking enabled flag

get.summary: Get email tracking events for a receipt

post.description: Voids a Creada receipt by creating a compensating credit note (NC). For WS receipts: NC must be authorized at AFIP before the original is marked Anulado. For pre-impreso: original is marked Anulado immediately. WS receipts always require a credit note (numbers cannot be reused).

200.description: Voided receipt with optional credit note ID

400.description: Validation failed. Codes: ORGANIZATION_ARCHIVED, RECEIPT_ALREADY_VOIDED, INVALID_RECEIPT_STATUS_FOR_VOID, RECEIPT_BOOK_NOT_FOUND, WS_VOID_REQUIRES_CREDIT_NOTE, RECEIPT_ALREADY_CREDITED, PARTIAL_CREDIT_EXISTS.

get.summary: List active tax categories for the current organization
Check if tax category is exportación
Check if tax category is monotributo

get.description: Look up a taxpayer to pre-fill a new customer form (fiscal name, tax category, address) or to verify an existing customer's details before issuing a receipt.

parameters.description: CUIT (companies) or CUIL (individuals) — 11 digits, no dashes
parameters.name: taxId

404.description: No taxpayer registered for this CUIT/CUIL

get.description: Call this before confirming a receipt to know whether you must issue a Factura de Crédito Electrónica (FCE). Pass the receipt's total and the customer's CUIT — when `isFceReceiver` is `true`, switch the receipt type to an FCE class so AFIP doesn't reject it.

parameters.description: Customer's CUIT (companies) or CUIL (individuals) — 11 digits, no dashes
parameters.name: taxId

parameters.description: The receipt's total in ARS. Omit (or `0`) to check whether the taxpayer can ever receive FCE, ignoring the amount.
parameters.name: total

200.description: `{ taxId, total, isFceReceiver }` — use `isFceReceiver` to decide the receipt type

404.description: No registry entry for this CUIT/CUIL

get.summary: Check FCE eligibility for a taxpayer

tags.description: Look up the AFIP tax category (condición frente al IVA) you assign to a customer or to your own organization — Responsable Inscripto, Monotributo, Consumidor Final, Exento, Exportación, etc. The list is filtered to the categories valid for your organization. Use the `/monotributo` and `/exportacion` flag endpoints to detect categories that change invoicing rules — Monotributo hides IVA discrimination, Exportación routes receipts through the WSFEX export flow.
tags.name: Common - Categorías Fiscales

tags.description: Manage the AFIP certificates your organization needs so AFIP can authorize the receipts you issue. Standard flow: `POST /certificates` with a `name`, download the generated CSR via `GET /certificates/:id/csr`, upload it to AFIP to obtain a `.crt`, post that file back via `POST /certificates/:id/upload-crt`, then call `POST /certificates/:id/validate-connection` to confirm AFIP accepts it. Keep an eye on `GET /certificates/expiration-status` so you can renew before a certificate expires — once it does, new receipts will pile up in `PENDIENTE_AFIP` (created but unauthorized) until you have a valid certificate again.
tags.name: Common - Certificados

tags.description: Look up shared option lists you need to fill dropdowns and pick valid values when creating other resources — receipt types, sale conditions, IVA aliquots, and similar enums. Fetch the items for a combo, show them to the user, and send the picked id when creating a receipt or customer. The IVA combo is auto-filtered to match your organization's tax regime, so you don't need to pre-filter it client-side.
tags.name: Common - Combos

tags.description: Get the exchange rate to use when invoicing in a foreign currency. Pass the source and target currency ids (and optionally a date) and use the rate the API returns. The response is `null` when no rate is available for that day — fall back to a manual rate in your UI when that happens.
tags.name: Common - Cotizaciones

tags.description: Download files the API generated for you asynchronously (CSV/Excel exports, large report renderings). Pass the `key` you received from the export endpoint to get back a signed URL valid for one hour — fetch the file from that URL within the hour. You can only download files that belong to your own organization.
tags.name: Common - Exportaciones

tags.description: Bulk-import customers, products, and other resources from Excel/CSV files. Create a file import to upload a file and kick off a background job, then poll the import to track progress and download the result file (success rows + error rows) once it finishes. Imports go through the same validation as the standard create endpoints, so successful rows are equivalent to creating each resource by hand.
tags.name: Common - Importaciones

tags.description: Look up Argentine provinces/regions and selected international destinations to fill the location field on customers, branches, and receipts. By default you get Argentine locations; pass `onlyExport=true` when issuing export invoices. The default location returned by `GET /locations/default` is the right pre-selection for new customer/branch forms.
tags.name: Common - Localidades

tags.description: Look up the currencies available for invoicing and reporting. Use `GET /currencies/favorites` to get the short list shown in dropdowns and `GET /currencies/default` to get the right pre-selection for new receipts (typically ARS).
tags.name: Common - Monedas

tags.description: Look up an Argentine taxpayer by CUIT/CUIL to pre-fill customer details (fiscal name, tax category, address) and to check whether you must issue a Factura de Crédito Electrónica (FCE). Call `GET /taxpayer-registries/:taxId/fce-receiver?total=…` before confirming a receipt — when `isFceReceiver` is true, switch the receipt type to an FCE class to avoid AFIP rejections.
tags.name: Common - Padrón

tags.description: Manage the physical branches/stores that issue receipts. Create one per location you operate from, then reference its id when creating receipts or assigning point-of-sale numbers. Branch names must be unique within the organization. Deleting a branch removes it from your active list while keeping past receipts linked to it.
tags.name: Common - Sucursales

tags.description: Manage your customer master data — fiscal name, tax id, tax category, billing address, contact details. Customers are referenced by id from sales receipts; create or update one before issuing a receipt to a new buyer. The CSV export streams cursor-based, so you can pull the full customer book in a single request.
tags.name: Ventas - Clientes

tags.description: Issue and manage sales receipts — facturas A/B/C/E, notas de crédito, notas de débito, recibos, FCE/MiPyME. Standard flow: create the receipt as a draft, add line details and observations, then call `POST :id/confirm` to assign the next sequential number from the receipt book and trigger AFIP authorization. Drafts can be edited or deleted freely; once confirmed, the only way to reverse a receipt is by issuing a credit note. Bulk endpoints (email, PDF zip, void, mark collected) run as background jobs you poll for progress.
tags.name: Ventas - Comprobantes

tags.description: Pull the sales reports you need to close the month and feed downstream tools — Libro IVA Ventas, sales by product, sales by customer, outstanding receivables, IIBB by jurisdiction, dashboard KPIs, revenue trends with MoM/YoY, and the IVA Digital Ventas fixed-width exports. Most reports have both a JSON endpoint (in-app views) and a CSV export endpoint (for accountants).
tags.name: Ventas - Reportes

tags.description: Manage the receipt books (talonarios / puntos de venta) you issue receipts from. Each book is tied to a branch, a letter (A/B/C), and a point-of-sale number; sequential numbering is tracked per book. Use `POST /receipt-books/import-from-arca` to pull your registered points of sale directly from AFIP instead of entering them by hand.
tags.name: Ventas - Talonarios

tags.description: Group products under categories so they can be filtered in lists, reports, and the product picker. Categories are scoped to your organization.
tags.name: Stock - Categorías

tags.description: Add manual stock adjustments and review the movement history for a product. Sales receipts and purchase receipts emit stock movements automatically; manual adjustments cover physical counts, breakage, and corrections. The history endpoint returns every movement for the product (manual + automatic) so current stock can be reconciled against its source events.
tags.name: Stock - Movimientos

tags.description: Manage your product catalog — sku, name, default price, IVA aliquot, category, stock-tracking flags. Products are referenced by id from sales-receipt detail lines and from stock movements. The search endpoint blends favorites, recently-used products, and fuzzy matching so the autocomplete in your UI returns relevant results fast.
tags.name: Stock - Productos

tags.description: Group purchase receipts under categories (rubros) so they can be filtered in purchase reports and feed accounting. Categories are scoped to your organization. Deletes are soft, so historical receipts keep their category context.
tags.name: Compras - Categorías

tags.description: Record the supplier invoices (facturas, notas de crédito, recibos) you receive. Capture the supplier id, document type and number, line details, and totals; the API computes IVA and net amounts. The unpaid-by-supplier endpoint returns balances per supplier so you can build payment orders.
tags.name: Compras - Comprobantes

tags.description: Manage your supplier master data — fiscal name, CUIT, tax category, payment terms. Suppliers are referenced by id from purchase receipts and payment orders. Deletes are soft, so historical receipts keep their supplier context.
tags.name: Compras - Proveedores

tags.description: Manage manual journal entries and read computed financial views over the journal — Profit & Loss for a date range and Balance Sheet at a point in time. Most journal entries are created automatically by the sales, purchase, and finance modules; this endpoint covers the manual adjustments your accountant posts at month-end.
tags.name: Accounting - Asientos

tags.description: Lock past accounting periods so journal entries with dates inside the lock window can't be created or modified. Use locks to freeze the books after a period close — adjustments must then be posted in a later period.
tags.name: Accounting - Bloqueos

tags.description: Manage fiscal years (ejercicios contables). Create one per fiscal period with start/end dates, then call `POST :id/close` once the period is done so reports stop including in-progress entries and locks can be enforced over closed periods.
tags.name: Accounting - Ejercicios

tags.description: Manage the chart of accounts (plan de cuentas) — the tree of accounts every journal entry posts against. Set the code, name, type (asset/liability/equity/income/expense), and parent account; entries automatically aggregate up the tree in reports.
tags.name: Accounting - Plan de Cuentas

tags.description: Pull the formal accounting reports your accountant signs off on — Libro Mayor (general ledger with running balances), Libro Diario (chronological journal), and Balance de Sumas y Saldos (trial balance). Each report has both a JSON endpoint for in-app views and a CSV export endpoint.
tags.name: Accounting - Reportes

tags.description: Manage the list of banks your organization operates with. Bank entries are referenced by id from bank accounts, third-party checks, and own checks.
tags.name: Finanzas - Bancos

tags.description: Manage cash registers (cajas) — physical or virtual cash drawers that hold money. Cash registers are referenced by id from fund movements; the histories endpoint returns the running balance and movement audit trail per register.
tags.name: Finanzas - Cajas

tags.description: Manage your own checkbooks. Each checkbook tracks the issuing bank, account, and number range; own checks issued from the book draw down its sequential numbers.
tags.name: Finanzas - Chequeras

tags.description: Manage third-party checks you receive from customers. Track the bank, number, due date, and current state (en cartera, depositado, endosado, rechazado, etc.). Checks are referenced from sales-receipt payment methods, deposits, and endorsements.
tags.name: Finanzas - Cheques

tags.description: Manage your own checks issued to suppliers. Each check is drawn from a checkbook (which tracks sequential numbering) and references the receiving party.
tags.name: Finanzas - Cheques Propios

tags.description: Manage the bank accounts your organization operates. Each account is tied to a bank and a currency; deposits, transfers, and bank movements reference the account by id. The histories endpoint returns the per-account movement log with running balance.
tags.name: Finanzas - Cuentas Bancarias

tags.description: Manage check deposits to a bank account. A deposit groups one or more third-party checks (en cartera) and produces a fund movement on the destination account so balances stay in sync.
tags.name: Finanzas - Depósitos

tags.description: Manage the payment methods (cash, card, transfer, check, MP, etc.) your organization accepts and the per-method entries that capture provider-specific configuration (acquirer fees, settlement days, etc.). Payment methods are referenced from receipts when recording how a sale was collected.
tags.name: Finanzas - Medios de Pago

tags.description: Manage fund movements — the generic ledger of money moving in and out of cash registers and bank accounts. Most movements are created automatically by other modules (receipts, deposits, transfers); this endpoint covers direct adjustments and the read view of the movement log.
tags.name: Finanzas - Movimientos de Fondos

tags.description: Manage card payments (cobros con tarjeta) — captured at sale time, settled by the acquirer days later. The cancellations endpoint returns voided card payments so reports can net them out.
tags.name: Finanzas - Pagos con Tarjeta

tags.description: Manage tax retentions (IVA, ganancias, IIBB, SUSS) you apply to supplier payments and the retention receipts (constancias de retención) you issue. Retention receipts are linked to the underlying purchase payment so reports can roll them up by period.
tags.name: Finanzas - Retenciones

tags.description: Manage the credit-card master data (issuer, brand, BIN, settlement plan) used by card-payment entries. The catalog drives dropdowns when recording a sale paid by card.
tags.name: Finanzas - Tarjetas de Crédito

tags.description: Manage bank transfers — money moving between two of your own accounts/registers. A transfer produces two fund movements (one debit, one credit) so balances stay in sync.
tags.name: Finanzas - Transferencias

tags.description: Manage Mercado Pago integrations — the OAuth tokens, refresh tokens, and account ids your organization uses to charge customers via MP. Use the expiring-soon endpoint to surface configs that need re-auth before tokens lapse, and `POST :id/reset-auth` to clear a refresh token after a revocation.
tags.name: Integraciones - Mercado Pago
